title: Origin Stories on my Website Tour - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a tour of the origin stories section of his website, explaining how he has organized 47 origin stories from 13 countries into a text-heavy layout optimized for text-to-speech computer reading. He discusses the scale of the content (24,528 pages), his chapter/column menu system, and his use of Flickr for images.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Everyone should memorize world origin stories; the human brain is capable of it and it's worth the effort
His design is the best in the world for computer-assisted reading due to maximizing text per page
Flickr is a good hosting solution because it doesn't slow down page load times

claims.claim: I like the setup I have with audio books, it's really good for history books because you can put the maps on either side of what you're talking about.

claims.subject: Flickr
claims.claim: Flickr has cool pictures to use and they don't make the page slower to load because they're hosted on another site.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to collect the most important origin stories, the biggest ones that are the basic ones of all the world.

claims.subject: Kyle's organization
claims.claim: I organize my origin stories with a five column menu list.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My favorite origin story is her out of Tissis Mysteries.

claims.subject: Kyle's system
claims.claim: If you wanted to make your own audio files, you could copy and paste pages to make your own using my system.
